supporting_works.title: Cooley's cyclopaedia of practical receipts and collateral information in the arts, manufactures, professions, and trades, including medicine, pharmacy, hygiene, and domestic economy : designed as a comprehensive supplement to the pharmacopoeia and general book of reference for the manufacturer, tradesman, amateur, and heads of families.

The pharmacopoeia universalis or, complete encyclopedia of the materia medica; contained in the pharmacopoeias of London, Edinburgh, and Dublin as well as of all those of Europe and America ; and of the dispensatories, formularies and chemical works ... With an appendix ... / by A. J. L. Jourdan : Edited by J. Rennie.

Gray's supplement to the Pharmacopoeia : being a concise but comprehensive dispensatory and manual of facts and formulae, for the chemist and druggist and medical practitioner / by Theophilus Redwood.

The Edinburgh new dispensatory : Containing I. The elements of pharmaceutical chemistry. II. The materia medica; or, The natural, pharmaceutical and medical history, of the substances employed in medicine. III. The pharmaceutical preparations and compositions. Including translations of the latest editions of the London, Edinburgh, and Dublin pharmacopoeias / by Andrew Duncan.

Gray's supplement to the pharmacopoeia : being a concise but comprehensive dispensatory and manual of facts and formulae, for the chemist and druggist and medical practitioner / by Theophilus Redwood.

The Edinburgh new dispensatory : containing 1. The elements of pharmaceutical chemistry. 2. The materia medica ... 3. The pharmaceutical preparations and compositions ; including translations of the London pharmacopoeia of 1825 ... / By Andrew Duncan.

The Edinburgh new dispensatory : containing, I. The elements of pharmaceutical chemistry: II. The materia medica, or, The natural, pharmaceutical, and medical history, of the substances employed in medicine: III. The pharmaceutical preparations and compositions including translations of the Edinburgh Pharmacopoeia published in 1817, of the Dublin Pharmacopoeia in 1807, and of the London Pharmacopoeia in 1815. Illustrated and explained in the languages, and according to the principles, of modern chemistry. With numerous tables / by Andrew Duncan, Jun.

The Edinburgh new dispensatory containing 1. The elements of pharmaceutical chemistry. 2. The materia medica ... 3. The pharmaceutical preparations and compositions. Including translations of the London pharmacopoeia of 1825 ... Edinburgh pharmacopoeia ... 1817; Dublin pharmacopoeia ... 1807 / By Andrew Duncan.
The elements of materia medica and therapeutics / by Jonathan Pereira.
A manual of pharmacy / by William Thomas Brande.

retrieval_text: Caraway is a culinary medicinal plant profile in the Herbalisti historical corpus. It is represented across 313 works and 1092 source-linked chunks. Recorded variants include Caraway, Caraways. Common plant-part co-mentions include Seed, Root, Leaf, Berry, Flower, Bark. Common preparation co-mentions include Oil, Powder, Syrup, Pill, Tincture, Extract. Common condition co-mentions include Cold or catarrh, Colic, Diarrhoea or looseness, Cough, Wounds or ulcers, Fever. Caution co-mentions include Dose caution, Narcotic or sedative caution, Child-use caution, Toxicity watch, Allergy or sensitivity caution, Bleeding caution. Representative source works include Cooley's cyclopaedia of practical receipts and collateral information in the arts, manufactures, professions, and trades, including medicine, pharmacy, hygiene, and domestic economy : designed as a comprehensive supplement to the pharmacopoeia and general book of reference for the manufacturer, tradesman, amateur, and heads of families.; The pharmacopoeia universalis or, complete encyclopedia of the materia medica; contained in the pharmacopoeias of London, Edinburgh, and Dublin as well as of all those of Europe and America ; and of the dispensatories, formularies and chemical works ... With an appendix ... / by A. J. L. Jourdan : Edited by J. Rennie.; Gray's supplement to the Pharmacopoeia : being a concise but comprehensive dispensatory and manual of facts and formulae, for the chemist and druggist and medical practitioner / by Theophilus Redwood.; The Edinburgh new dispensatory : Containing I. The elements of pharmaceutical chemistry. II. The materia medica; or, The natural, pharmaceutical and medical history, of the substances employed in medicine. III. The pharmaceutical preparations and compositions. Including translations of the latest editions of the London, Edinburgh, and Dublin pharmacopoeias / by Andrew Duncan.. This is a retrieval summary of historical co-mentions only, not a medical claim.
